site.name: Serbay Sivrikaya
site.tagline: I design interfaces for products where the work is in the details.
site.description: Portfolio of Serb, a UI/UX designer focused on product clarity in fintech, dev tools, and B2B SaaS.
site.availability: Available for select projects from August 2026.

hero.headline: Clarity at every pixel.
hero.subheadline: I help product teams turn dense, technical software into interfaces people actually want to use. From zero to online.

intro.title: I design for technical products. I build them too.
intro.body: Most of my work sits at the intersection of engineering and design — data-heavy dashboards, dev tools, financial products. I care about information density, motion that explains rather than decorates, and the kind of typography hierarchy that lets a user find the one important number in a screen full of them. I don't just hand off mockups — I write the production front-end code.

about.title: Designer, not decorator. Developer, not just implementer.
about.lead: I'm Serb — a UI/UX designer with a background in front-end engineering. The two disciplines shape how I work: I think in systems, ship in components, and write copy that respects the reader. I don't just hand off mockups — I build the thing.

sections.heading: How I work
sections.body: I join projects early, before the pixel decisions get locked. I map the problem with the team, draft the structure in low-fi, then push high-fi only when the structure is solid. Reviews happen on a shared Figma file — no PDFs, no slides.

sections.heading: What I care about
sections.body: Density, legibility, motion that earns its place, and copy that tells the user what is happening without making them feel stupid. I’ve been told I have strong opinions about kerning.

experience.summary: Working with seed-to-Series-B teams on fintech, dev tools, and B2B SaaS. Recent clients include a payments API, an observability platform, and a logistics dashboard. From zero to online.
Led design on the analytics surface, from initial concept to GA. Defined and maintained the design system used by 14 squads.
Worked across agency clients: banking apps, healthcare portals, e-commerce. Learned what ‘done’ means in 12 different industries.
Started in code, drifted into design. The path still informs everything I do — every mockup I draw is one I could ship.

contact.title: Have a project in mind?
contact.lead: I take on a small number of engagements per quarter. Tell me about the product, the team, and the rough timeline — I’ll reply within two working days.

process.footer: I run the full loop — research, design, and the production front-end code — so the artifact you see above is the artifact you ship.
